market_pulse.one_liner: Index-level gamma support masks underlying breadth deterioration and risk-off rotation.

gamma_landscape.notes: Positive GEX at 730 provides a structural floor, but the massive negative DEX and VEX suggest high tail-risk sensitivity if the 730 level is breached.

risks: Dealer gamma flip if SPY drops below 730
Breadth deterioration as small-caps (IWM) and financials (XLF) lag
High VEX indicating hidden tail-risk hedging
Low model accuracy necessitates strict position sizing
disagreements: Quantitative models (GEX) favor a bullish drift/pinning, while fundamental and sentiment models flag a risk-off regime and breadth decay.
narrative: The market is currently caught in a structural trap where bullish dealer positioning at the 730 strike creates a false sense of stability, masking a broader risk-off rotation. While the gamma profile supports a grind toward the 730 pin, the underlying deterioration in small-caps and financials suggests the index is vulnerable to a violent unwind if the 730 support fails. We prioritize capital preservation and limit new exposure to a single, tightly-stopped SPY position to capitalize on the gamma pin while respecting the broader risk-off signal.
